title: Micro Travel Grant Programme: this month's round is now open
excerpt: #ScienceForUkraine's Micro Travel Grant Programme opens a new application round every month, supporting early-career scholars based in Ukraine with in-person conference participation.
body:
A new monthly round of the Micro Travel Grant (MTG) Programme is now open. MTG supports early-career scholars based in Ukraine to participate in international academic conferences, workshops, and symposia in person.

Who can apply: doctoral candidates and researchers within five years of their PhD, based in Ukraine and affiliated with a Ukrainian academic institution, with an accepted in-person conference presentation.

What is supported: reimbursement of eligible costs up to EUR 500, covering travel, accommodation, and conference fees.

The programme is entirely volunteer-run, and 100% of donations go directly to grants supporting young Ukrainian scholars.

title: MTG 2026 awardees: February-April round
excerpt: We are pleased to announce the Micro Travel Grant awardees for February-April 2026, supported to attend international conferences in archaeology, chemistry, and biology.
body:
We are pleased to announce the MTG2026 awardees of the #ScienceForUkraine Micro Travel Grant Programme for February-April 2026.

Through the programme, early-career researchers based in Ukraine receive support for participation in international academic conferences and scientific exchange.

**Round 1 (February 2026):** **Iryna Chechulina**, Department of Classical Archaeology, Institute of Archaeology, National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine, to attend the 32nd Annual Meeting of the European Association of Archaeologists.

**Round 2 (March 2026):** **Marharyta Cherniakova**, Institute of Functional Materials Chemistry, Institute for Single Crystals, National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine, to attend Supramol2026: XVII Italian Conference on Supramolecular Chemistry.

**Round 3 (April 2026):** **Yaroslava Bukhonska**, V.P. Kukhar Institute of Bioorganic Chemistry and Petrochemistry, National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine, to attend SymBioSE: 29th Symposium of Biology Students in Europe.

title: UK-Ukraine Academic Mentoring Programme: June newsletter
excerpt: The programme's latest newsletter rounds up the Liverpool networking event, mentoring opportunities, funding calls, and PhD application guidance for Ukrainian researchers.
body:
The June 2026 UK-Ukraine Academic Mentoring Programme newsletter aims to foster academic solidarity, provide practical support, and promote international research collaboration.

Research Twinning Showcase and Networking 2026: a recap of the University of Liverpool event, which brought together over 80 academics, policymakers, and industry leaders from the UK and Ukraine to strengthen research partnerships.

Mentoring Programme: the UK-Ukraine Academic Mentoring Programme connects Ukrainian researchers and PhD students with UK mentors, supporting professional development, collaboration, and long-term academic partnerships during ongoing disruptions in Ukraine.

Opportunities & Funding: a wide range of grants, fellowships, and travel awards for researchers at all career stages, alongside step-by-step guidance for applying to UK PhD programmes and personal stories from Ukrainian academics on the impact of mentorship and collaboration.

title: Presenting the Micro Travel Grant Programme to the Ukrainian Science Diaspora in Spain
excerpt: Galyna Ryabukha presented #ScienceForUkraine's Micro Travel Grant Programme at an info session on 2026 grant opportunities organised by the Ukrainian Science Diaspora community in Spain.
body:
The Info Session: Grant Opportunities 2026 was organised by the Ukrainian Science Diaspora community in Spain, bringing together speakers Olesia Skliarenko and Oleksandr Smyrnov for an insightful session on the New European Bauhaus and Twinning initiatives for institutional partnerships.

Galyna Ryabukha represented #ScienceForUkraine at the session, presenting the Micro Travel Grant (MTG) Programme to attendees. MTG supports the integration of early-career Ukrainian scholars into the international research community through in-person participation in international events, covering eligible costs up to EUR 500.

Since the programme began, MTG has been facilitated in its dissemination and implementation by the BETA Technological Centre (Universitat de Vic - Universitat Central de Catalunya).

title: UK-Ukraine Research Twinning Showcase and Networking 2026
excerpt: Over 80 academics, policymakers, and industry leaders from the UK and Ukraine gathered at the University of Liverpool to strengthen research partnerships and support Ukraine's integration into the global academic community.
body:
The UK-Ukraine Research Twinning Showcase and Networking 2026 took place at the University of Liverpool on 23-24 March 2026, bringing together more than 80 academics, policymakers, and industry leaders from the UK and Ukraine.

The event focused on strengthening research partnerships, sharing experiences, and supporting Ukraine's integration into the global academic community, aligning closely with #ScienceForUkraine's own mission of keeping Ukrainian researchers connected internationally.

The showcase forms part of the wider UK-Ukraine Research Twinning and Academic Mentoring effort, which connects Ukrainian researchers and PhD students with UK-based mentors for long-term collaboration.

title: Four years of war. Four years of #ScienceForUkraine.
excerpt: On the fourth anniversary of the full-scale invasion, #ScienceForUkraine reflects on its journey from a grassroots initiative to a volunteer-run NGO supporting Ukraine's academic community.
body:
Four years of Russia's full-scale war against Ukraine. Four years of #ScienceForUkraine.

We began as a grassroots initiative with a single tweet four years ago. Since then, we have grown into an NGO, but our mission remains the same: to support Ukrainian scholars and their connection to the international academic community.

One way we do this today is through our Micro Travel Grants programme, supporting early-career researchers based in Ukraine to attend conferences, build collaborations, and remain part of global science.

Despite loss and disruption, Ukrainian researchers and students continue their work. We stand with them and remain committed to supporting Ukraine's academic community. Science knows no borders, and neither does our solidarity.
